concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: All manifested titles and complete abstracts for batch records assigned to ensemble_forecasting and machine_learning_forecasting.
concept_decisions.rationale: The retained cluster is specifically multimodel or multi-member forecasting; the only machine_learning_forecasting paper with that design is reassigned here.

concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: All manifested titles and complete abstracts for batch records assigned to machine_learning_forecasting and adjacent predictive-method records in the manifest.
concept_decisions.rationale: This bucket mixes one ensemble-forecast study with non-ensemble predictive warning models. The non-ensemble predictive designs cohere as a separate concept centered on indicator-, biomarker-, threshold-, or ML-based warning.

concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: All manifested titles and complete abstracts for batch records assigned to forecasting_dynamics_models and neighboring manifest concepts.
concept_decisions.rationale: The provisional bucket combines transmission inference, surveillance systems, molecular surveillance, program evaluation, and predictive warning models. Those are distinct methodological designs and need separation.

concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: All manifested titles and complete abstracts for batch records assigned to outbreak_anomaly_detection and related forecasting_dynamics_models records.
concept_decisions.rationale: This bucket conflates core anomaly-detection algorithms with surveillance systems, molecular typing/genomics, and operational program evaluations. The algorithm lineage is retained separately and the rest are redistributed by design.

new_concepts.rationale: Groups non-ensemble predictive warning designs that derive alerts from ML, biomarkers, thresholds, traveler surveillance, or other prospective indicators.
Groups studies that estimate epidemic growth, reproductive numbers, serial intervals, spread, or intervention effects from observational data or mechanistic formulations.
Groups operational surveillance platforms, wastewater or sentinel monitoring systems, digital reporting infrastructures, and implemented early-warning architectures.
Groups molecular, sequence-based, and genomic surveillance designs used to resolve clusters, support traceback, or enhance outbreak recognition.
Groups studies that evaluate detection timeliness, coverage, workflow, preparedness, or field performance of real-world outbreak detection programs rather than proposing a new algorithm.
Groups papers whose primary contribution is an automated anomaly, scan, threshold, fusion, or comparative detection method.
